articles.title: Nigeria’s online content creator market has boomed. Can the skit-makers and streamers make it pay?
articles.content:
As platforms make less from advertising, creators are struggling to monetise work – leading to calls for more government investment and tax breaks

On a humid afternoon in Lagos, a shoot for a comedy skit is under way on a set that looks more like a small film production.

Dozens of people mill about: lighting assistants, a sound engineer, a makeup artist and even a content creator recording unscripted behind-the-scenes footage. At the centre is Broda Shaggi, born Samuel Animashaun Perry, who is issuing instructions, rehearsing lines and performing caricatures.

Continue reading...

articles.title: Cory Booker calls both parties ‘feckless’ for ceding war powers to Trump
articles.content:
Democrat says Congress ‘doing nothing’ may embolden president to attack countries such as Cuba and North Korea

Democratic US senator Cory Booker has criticized both his own political party as well as its Republican counterpart for being “feckless” in ceding congressional war powers to Donald Trump, saying that their decision could embolden the president to unilaterally attack Cuba, North Korea and other countries.

“I’m going to be one of those Democrats [who] say I think both parties have been feckless in allowing the growth of the power of the presidency,” Booker said on Sunday on CNN’s State of the Union.

Continue reading...

articles.content: Volodymyr Zelensky says restoring the flow of Russian oil via Ukraine into the EU would be like lifting sanctions on Russia.

articles.content: Pakistani airstrikes have killed at least 75 civilians and displaced 115,000 in Afghanistan, with both sides vowing escalation and no talks in sight.

articles.content: Voters across France choose mayors this week. The far right is performing strongly in the country’s second city, making the contest there a test of national shifts.

articles.title: As Rockets Fly Overhead, Residents of Israel’s Border City Stay Underground
articles.content: The Israeli government evacuated Kiryat Shmona during the last round of fighting with Hezbollah in 2023. Residents who were told it was safe to return are again under fire.

articles.content: Helgi Hjorleifsson, a firefighter, is a leader in a national experiment to steer rivers of lava away from important sites. Some called it crazy, but it worked.

articles.title: Madagascar anti-corruption chief appointed as new PM
articles.content: Madagascar's strongman leader has appointed Mamitiana Rajaonarison as PM days after dismissing the last government. Rajaonarison previously led the country's financial intelligence and money laundering watchdog.

articles.title: Hungary's rival parties hold parallel rallies ahead of vote
articles.content: Viktor Orban is facing his first serious challenge in years in Peter Magyar. The two politicians held rallies on Hungary's national day that outlined very different visions for the country's future.

articles.title: War, oppression and fear: In Iran, traumas are accumulating
articles.content: Bombs, government oppression and fear are wearing down Iranians. How does trauma accumulate? And what can help people endure constant stress?

articles.title: Kosovo faces third legislative election in just over a year
articles.content: Kosovo’s failure to elect a president has again plunged the nation into political deadlock. Now, it could face its third parliamentary election in just over a year. The country's international reputation is taking a hit.

articles.title: Iran says women's soccer captain drops Australia asylum bid
articles.content: Five of the seven women who had been granted asylum in Australia have reportedly decided to return to Iran. Concerns about their safety arose after players remained silent during the Iranian national anthem.

articles.title: Israeli troops kill 4 in Palestinian family in West Bank
articles.content: A Palestinian family of four has been killed after Israeli forces opened fire on a vehicle in the occupied West Bank. In Gaza, a family of three and a teenage neighbor were killed in an apparent Israeli strike.

articles.title: Civil war in Sudan is a never-ending humanitarian crisis
articles.content: Almost three years into Sudan's war, drone attacks and sexual violence show no signs of relenting. Could intensified international efforts influence the situation?

articles.title: Razzie Awards 2026: Anti-Oscars name ‘War Of The Worlds’ as Worst Picture
articles.content: Worst Picture was just one of five awards that 'War Of The Worlds' took home, making it something of a clean sweep for the Ice Cube-starring adaptation / devastation of H.G. Wells’ classic novel.

articles.title: EU Parliament firewall breached? EPP reportedly worked closely with AfD
articles.content: Investigations suggest the EPP group in the European Parliament cooperated more closely with right-wing parties, including the AfD, on tougher EU migration laws, with a chat group and a meeting of MEPs playing a key role.

articles.title: EU weighs extending naval mission Aspides to Strait of Hormuz amid Iran crisis
articles.content: EU foreign ministers meeting in Brussels on Monday are set to discuss extending the bloc's naval mission Aspides to the Strait of Hormuz, as the Iran war pushed global energy prices up. Over the weekend, US President Trump called on several countries to send ships to secure the passage.

articles.title: 'Orbán betrayed Hungarian freedom': Magyar accuses PM of inviting Russian agents to rig the vote
articles.content: Opposition leader Péter Magyar called Viktor Orbán a traitor at a Budapest rally, accusing him of enlisting Russian agents to interfere in Hungary's 6 April elections, as polls show Tisza ahead of Fidesz.

articles.title: Latest news bulletin | March 15th, 2026 – Evening

articles.title: 'Our sons will not die for Ukraine,' Orbán tells supporters ahead of crucial April elections
articles.content: Hungarian PM Viktor Orbán rallied supporters in Budapest, framing April's election as a choice between peace and war, while lashing out at Ukraine's Zelenskyy and the EU over the Druzhba pipeline dispute.

articles.title: Zelenskyy says ready for another round of peace talks but waiting on US and Russia
articles.content: US-led peace talks between Ukraine and Russia had been under way in the United Arab Emirates but were placed on hold amid the Iran war.

articles.title: North Korea conducts test of nuclear-capable rocket launchers
articles.content: South Korea’s military said Saturday it detected about 10 ballistic missiles fired from North Korea’s capital region toward the eastern sea. The launch coincides with US-South Korean Freedom Shield training, a computer-simulated command post exercise.

articles.title: Starmer speaks to Trump about importance of reopening Strait of Hormuz

articles.title: 'No Clear Off-Ramp': Asia Hit Hard by Hormuz Blockade
articles.content: Asian economies face mounting pressure as the Strait of Hormuz remains closed and President Trump weighs potential negotiations. Bloomberg’s Weilun Soon breaks down the latest oil disruptions. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Oil Erases Gains as Focus on Physical Supply After Kharg Attack
articles.content: Oil erased early gains as traders sought to gauge the impact to supply after US attacks on Iran’s main export hub, another escalation in the war that’s all but cut off global customers from the region’s energy supplies for more than two weeks.

articles.title: Asian Stocks Edge Up as Oil Declines, Dollar Falls: Markets Wrap
articles.content: Asian stocks advanced after oil erased its earlier gains as markets attempted to stabilize following the US strike on Iran’s main export hub. The dollar also weakened.

articles.title: Munitions Metal Tungsten Eclipses Gold, Copper in 557% Rally
articles.content: A metal used in weapons and semiconductors is finding itself the subject of geopolitical tensions as Chinese export limits and rising military demand squeeze supplies, sending prices to record highs.

articles.title: Taiwan Swaps Signal Rate Hike Bets on Inflation, Currency Risks
articles.content: Fixed-income investors are pricing in an interest-rate hike in Taiwan in the coming months as surging oil prices and a weakening currency spark inflation concerns.

articles.title: Gold Falls Below $5,000 as Mideast War Keeps Oil Prices Elevated
articles.content: Gold slid below $5,000 an ounce, as the war in the Middle East entered a third week and oil prices spiked after attacks on critical energy infrastructure over the weekend.

articles.title: Trump Floats Delaying Xi Summit If No Help for Hormuz, FT Says
articles.content: Donald Trump said he could delay his planned summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping if Beijing doesn’t help unblock the Strait of Hormuz, the Financial Times reported, citing an interview with the US president.

articles.title: China’s Power ‘Supergrid’ Gives Xi Buffer Against Energy Shocks
articles.content: China’s long-running effort to build out its energy sources is getting fresh momentum from the war in the Middle East, reinforcing a strategy that’s sent grid operators on a bond-selling binge and funneled hundreds of billions of dollars into the market.

articles.title: U.S. oil prices top $100 as Trump administration threatens strikes on Iran's crude export facilities
articles.content: The U.S. strikes on Kharg Island and Trump's threat to hit Iran's oil infrastructure mark a major escalation in the war, according to JPMorgan.

articles.title: Trump weighing options to strike Iran's critical oil hub, UN Ambassador Waltz says
articles.content: Israel says it has killed two senior Iranian intelligence officials as oil loading reportedly resumes in the UAE's Fujairah port following a fire.

articles.title: Asia-Pacific markets trade mixed as oil hovers near $100 amid escalating U.S.-Iran tensions
articles.content: Asia-Pacific markets traded mixed as investors continued to monitor elevated oil prices and the latest developments in the escalating U.S.-Iran conflict.

articles.title: Democrats blast FCC Chair Carr's broadcast license threats as anti-First Amendment, 'totalitarian'
articles.content: Carr on Saturday blasted broadcasters shortly after President Donald Trump called reports that Iran struck five U.S. tanker planes "fake news."

articles.title: Senate Democrats oppose SAVE America Act as Republicans prepare for floor vote. What to know
articles.content: Senate Democrats remain opposed to SAVE America Act as Republicans prepare for floor vote this week. Here's what to know.

articles.title: Why the United Arab Emirates is a target for Iran's aggression
articles.content: Iran is looking to test a state that has positioned itself as the Gulf's safest bridge between East and West — and the future of the region.

articles.title: Left-wing candidate Emmanuel Grégoire tops first round of Paris mayoral race, exit polls say
Socialist leads Paris mayoral race but leftist rival threatens to split vote
articles.content: Socialist candidate Emmanuel Grégoire surged ahead of former culture minister Rachida Dati in the first round of voting for Paris mayor on Sunday as the left bids to extend its 25 years at the helm. But in the capital as in other cities across France, a strong showing by Jean-Luc Mélenchon's France Unbowed (LFI) could complicate matters for the mainstream left.

articles.title: French far right eyes Marseille upset after strong showing in mayoral elections
articles.content: Candidates for the far-right National Rally posted strong results in the first round of France’s municipal elections on Sunday, winning several races outright and polling neck-and-neck with the incumbent left in Marseille, in the last major test of the French public mood ahead of next year’s all-important presidential election.

articles.title: Being an artist and a refugee: Amir Amiri and Parisa Karimi Molan speak to France 24
articles.content: France 24’s Gavin Lee speaks with Amir Amiri and Parisa Karimi, two Iranian musicians who now live in canada. They discuss the difficulties and hopes of being refugees and artists from a war-torn country.

articles.title: Turnout low in French mayoral elections seen as key test ahead of 2027 presidential race
articles.content: French voters cast ballots in municipal elections marred by low turnout on Sunday, in the last major test of the public mood ahead of next year’s all-important presidential election. The run-up to the vote was largely overshadowed by the Iran war and its fallout, notably the impact on fuel prices.

articles.title: From missiles strikes to rocketing oil price: Guy Laron speaks to France 24 to analyse the economic consequences of Iran war
articles.content: France 24’s Gavin Lee speaks with Guy Laron, Senior lecturer in international relations, the Hebrew University of Jerusalem, about the economic consequences of the ongoing war in Iran, from rising oil prices to how the strategic Strait of Hormuz can be used as an economic weapon.

articles.title: Trump administration threatens media outlets over Iran war coverage
articles.content: The head of the Federal Communications Commission, which oversees US media outlets, has warned that some broadcasters risk losing their licenses over their news coverage of the Iran war. Brendan Carr – a Trump appointee – wrote in a social media post that broadcasters must operate in the public interest and those that are running hoaxes and fake news have the chance now to correct course before their license renewals come up. Free speech groups blasted Carr's warning, calling it outrageous. Analysis by Philip Turle, international affairs editor.

articles.title: Severe sandstorm sweeps through Gaza tent camps
articles.content: Sandstorms swept through tent camps in southern and central Gaza, tearing down shelters and burying belongings under sand as thousands of displaced Palestinians struggled to hold onto their makeshift homes. Families in areas like Khan Younis and Gaza City say powerful winds repeatedly collapsed tents, worsening already dire living conditions after months of conflict. Meanwhile, violence has continued despite a ceasefire, with casualties reported in recent weeks.

articles.title: U.S. stock futures bounce back, oil falls as investors weigh developments in Iran conflict
articles.content: U.S. stock-index futures reversed early losses on Sunday as the market braced for another surge in oil prices this week, with the conflict with Iran threatening to escalate further.

articles.title: It was unthinkable a couple of weeks ago, but could the next move by the Fed be a rate hike?
articles.content: With fuel prices soaring since the start of the war with Iran, a question has surfaced that was almost unimaginable a few weeks ago.

articles.title: The financial sector is sending some spooky technical signals about the stock market
articles.content: Broken trendlines and a looming ‘death cross’ pattern bode badly for the financial sector, and therefore the rest of the stock market.

articles.title: Nvidia’s big GTC event is on deck, and the company faces a very high bar this year
articles.content: Nvidia “is having a harder time moving the needle” these days, one analyst notes.

articles.title: ‘It’s all about oil’: FedEx’s transportation costs will be key to this week’s earnings
articles.content: With results due this week from FedEx and retail names like Lululemon and Macy’s, we’ll likely hear more about consumers’ attitudes, as gas prices and shipping costs spike and the conflict widens in the Middle East.

articles.title: Individual investors are chasing oil’s surge amid Iran conflict; institutions are thinking about what comes next
articles.content: A popular oil fund pulled in $1 billion from investors in 9 days, while an ETF that tracks the S&P 500 shed $12.6 billion

articles.title: Panic is slowly gripping the stock market. Expect the selling to pick up this week.
articles.content: Options traders are signaling trouble, and systematic funds are expected to cut their exposure to U.S. stocks.

articles.title: Deportees sent by Trump to Salvadoran prison are still stuck a year later

articles.content: A split-screen response shows how Pope Leo is navigating a minefield as the Trump administration projects American nativism at home and deadly force overseas.
